page: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC processor. This server is configured with 8 shared vCPUs across 4 physical cores, supporting 2 threads per core at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. It includes a 32 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The hardware profile is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, scoring 3434.87 in stress-ng single-core tests, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory performance is also highly competitive, with a PassMark memory score in the top 10% and strong large-block memory read and write bandwidths of 247.43 GB/sec and 159.49 GB/sec, respectively. Multi-core performance, database operations, and Redis SET operations fall within the average tier, with Redis handling 21.69 million operations per second. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks show strong results, while medium-model LLM prompt processing represents a relative performance bottleneck.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 and shared CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-effective solution for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The combination of 64.0 GB of RAM and 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage makes it well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, in-memory data processing, and general-purpose web serving. However, the shared CPU allocation and average multi-core scaling suggest it is less optimal for sustained, heavy parallel computing or large-scale machine learning training.
description: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-400s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on an x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, the processor supports AVX-512 and SHA_NI instructions. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside strong large-block memory read and write bandwidths. Multi-core operations, database tasks, and Redis throughput align with average performance tiers. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8, this instance provides a resource-dense, cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy workloads. It is highly suitable for database hosting, caching layers, in-memory data processing, and web serving, though the shared CPU allocation represents a tradeoff for sustained parallel computing tasks.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-400s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered instance with 8 shared vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 400 GB NVMe storage, offering top-tier single-core and memory benchmark performance.

tagline: A memory-optimized AMD EPYC server featuring high RAM density and local NVMe storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC processor with 8 shared vCPUs and 64.0 GB RAM
400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
Strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth
Optimized for database hosting, caching layers, and in-memory processing
